title: To prohibit the use of Federal funds to support or facilitate the participation of the Russian Federation in the Group of Seven, and for other purposes.
plain_summary: This bill stops the US government from using any federal money to help Russia take part in Group of Seven meetings. The Group of Seven is a group of seven wealthy countries that meet to discuss world issues. Russia used to be part of this group when it was called the Group of Eight, but Russia was kicked out in 2014 after it took over part of Ukraine. The bill also prevents federal money from being used if the group becomes the Group of Eight again with Russia as a member.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.
plain_keywords: Group of Seven Russia kicked out

sections.title: Section 1 Prohibition on Russian Participation in the G7
sections.text: The United States has a policy to keep the Russian Federation out of the Group of Seven. The Group of Seven is a group of seven major countries that meet to discuss world economic issues. The United States will also not support creating a Group of Eight that includes Russia. No federal government money can be used to help Russia participate in Group of Seven meetings or activities. No federal money can be used to help create a new Group of Eight that includes Russia as a member.
